turns.assistant_text:
Here are some recent updates and information about Wayne Rooney:

1. **Wayne and Moyes: The Untold Story Of The Transfer That Changed Everything!**  
   In this episode of the Wayne Rooney Show, Rooney discusses his transfer from Everton to Manchester United with manager David Moyes. The episode covers secret meetings and emotional reflections on leaving his boyhood club. [Watch it here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UjGIEt7njkI).

2. **Inside Rooney's Transfer from Everton to Man Utd**  
   David Moyes shares insights on how Wayne Rooney's transfer to Manchester United came about. This video provides a brief overview of the transfer saga. [View the video](https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/videos/c794y90e88no).

3. **The Wayne Rooney Show: Premier League Team of the Season**  
   Rooney discusses his picks for the Premier League team of the season, highlighting standout players. [Check it out here](https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/videos/c172krrgznqo).

4. **Roy's Saipan Secrets & Man United's Go Kart Controversy**  
   Another episode of The Green Room features Wayne Rooney sharing stories and insights from his career. [Watch it here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1A975Ql0X3c).

5. **BIG Calls as Wayne Rooney Reveals Team of the Year**  
   Rooney reveals his Premier League Team of the Year, discussing why certain players made the cut. [Watch the video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UbcjShcAgDU).

### Recent Tweets:
- **Wayne Rooney's Class**  
  A tweet discussing Rooney's perspective on football and teamwork. [View tweet](https://x.com/MwakioAm/status/2061740353690935506).

- **Branching Out Beyond Football**  
  A tweet mentioning Rooney's ventures outside of football. [View tweet](https://x.com/Damdirtyape5/status/2061739643897569555).

- **Referee Controversy**  
  A discussion referencing Rooney's comments on a controversial match involving PSG. [View tweet](https://x.com/ibbourne/status/2061738728406597852).

These sources provide a comprehensive overview of Wayne Rooney's recent activities and discussions in the football world.

items.title: Wayne and Moyes: The Untold Story Of The Transfer That Changed Everything! | Wayne Rooney Show
items.summary:
Wayne and Moyes: The Untold Story Of The Transfer That Changed Everything! | Wayne Rooney Show
BBC Football
11600 subscribers
1264 likes
120385 views
29 May 2026
Wayne Rooney returns to Everton with the manager who gave him his debut, David Moyes. What really went on before Sir Alex finally signed Wayne - the Everton manager reveals all. Why did Everton really have to sell their biggest homegrown star? And would Wayne have done anything differently?

From secret transfer meetings and emotional reflections on leaving his boyhood club, to the untold stories behind Rooney’s explosive rise, this is a fascinating reunion between two men whose careers are forever linked

Plus, brutal old-school pre-seasons, brilliant dressing room stories, Everton’s revival under Moyes, and what do they both think of England’s and Scotland’s chances at the World Cup?.

Follow more on the BBC Sport app, iPlayer and Sounds.

00:00 Tease
00:50 Intro  
02:28 What was it like coming across Wayne Rooney?  
04:52 Working with Rooney  
06:47 Arriving at Everton in 2002  
09:39 Is the club the right fit for the manager?  
10:41 Wayne on leaving Everton  
16:07 Being tough on players then and now  
18:12 Knowing Wayne was going to leave  
21:08 What is David Moyes most proud of at Everton?  
22:42 How much has managing Premier League teams changed?  
24:33 How has David changed from then to now?  
24:37 Rooney’s pre-season antics  
28:10 What’s it like being back at Everton?  
32:01 Winning the Conference League with West Ham  
34:36 Has there been a mentality shift in Everton?  
37:37 World Cup chat

=============

items.title: @GistArsena99455 This is what I call Class. Football isn't a one team affair. All teams need each other to compete. So b
items.summary: @GistArsena99455 This is what I call Class. Football isn't a one team affair. All teams need each other to compete. So bitterness doesn't hold. Wayne Rooney is a rare smart fella.

items.title: @Mutape88 @W3Zhira @Phidze72 @SekuruRoddie @Simba_LionDRED @ignitive3_0 @tonde_tonderai @NtateRanaka @lashiasn @tino_cas
items.summary: @Mutape88 @W3Zhira @Phidze72 @SekuruRoddie @Simba_LionDRED @ignitive3_0 @tonde_tonderai @NtateRanaka @lashiasn @tino_cash3 @garweh2011 @joecharex @pfunguro68410 @babatafi @Shumblex @mukokwapashata1 @BeautyZhuwao @abuhassan221 @pfunguro57708 @kkele @vargoe21 @BlessingMombes3 @VimILSung @Renegad63094431 @BigYouuttZim @Lashian I am not a conspiracy theorist. I kept my thoughts to myself. However, when I read statements by people like Wayne Rooney, Arsene Wenger&Pettit, my mind was jolted. The question I have is-Are sovereign owned clubs-City and PSG-deploying sovereign diplomacy off the field?

items.title: @FootballFactly This must be a joke?
items.summary:
@FootballFactly This must be a joke?

He was wrong to blow before the corner kick was taken and spent the whole 2nd half blowing in favour of PSG while he counted the air miles and golf holidays coming his way from Qatar and its state-owned holding companies. As Wayne Rooney said, PSG stole it.

items.title: @Joe___Allen This is BS. The referee was wrong to blow before the corner kick was taken and spent the whole 2nd half blo
items.summary: @Joe___Allen This is BS. The referee was wrong to blow before the corner kick was taken and spent the whole 2nd half blowing in favour of PSG while he counted the air miles and golf holidays coming his way from Qatar and its state-owned holding companies. As Wayne Rooney said, PSG stole it.
